findings.title: HTTPS agent disables certificate validation
findings.evidence: raw-git-diff.patch adds `rejectUnauthorized: false` to the exported `agentOptions` object in src/httpClient.ts (lines 6-9). This disables certificate validation for outbound HTTPS, exposing all traffic through this agent to man-in-the-middle attacks. The branch name `agent/fix-staging-webhooks` suggests a staging convenience hack, but the change is unconditional and ships to all environments with no guard.

notes: The Diff Drift report accurately matches the raw diff: a single one-line addition to agentOptions. No discrepancy or unflagged change found across the packet.
Only one file changed and it was fully analyzed; nothing was skipped as too large.

`rejectUnauthorized` belongs on the TLS connection/agent options; placing it on this exported `agentOptions` object is meaningful only if the object is passed to an https.Agent or request — the surrounding usage is not shown in the packet but the security intent (disabling validation) is unambiguous.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 blind run against the engine-v2 binary; packet-only context.
